items.title: Former Kitchener, Ont., neurologist faces 2 new sex assault charges after acquittal on previous counts
items.summary_auto: Former Kitchener, Ont., neurologist Jeffrey Sloka is facing two new sexual assault charges, weeks after a judge found him not guilty of 48 previous counts. Crown prosecutors are appealing those acquittals.

items.title: Disney sued over new facial recognition technology at Disneyland entrances
items.summary_auto: The Walt Disney Company is facing a class-action lawsuit over a recently introduced use of facial recognition technology at its Disneyland theme park entrances.

items.title: Fans welcome Montreal Victoire home after historic Walter Cup win
items.summary_auto: The Montreal Victoire were met with chants and cheers from fans as the team arrived in Verdun following their victory in Ottawa.

items.title: 3 storylines to watch in a big PWHL off-season
items.summary_auto: Four new teams, a talented draft class and a roster reset all coming this summer are some of the key storylines to watch as the PWHL officially starts its off-season.

items.title: Hyundai recalls some Elantra Hybrid vehicles over possible overheating, fire risk
items.summary_auto: Recall applies to 6,082 Canadian vehicles, according to Hyundai. No fires, incidents, injuries or fatalities have been reported in Canada related to the issue.

items.title: Major system outage delayed boarding on international flights to Canada, CBSA confirms
items.summary_auto: The Canada Border Services Agency (CBSA) says a system outage that delayed travellers arriving in Canada earlier this week also caused delays for passengers boarding international flights bound for Canada.

items.title: Toronto police set up dedicated phone line in search for missing teen
items.summary_auto: Toronto police have set up a dedicated phone line to collect tips from the public as they intensify their search for a teen missing for six days.

items.title: Your slice of the bread price-fixing settlement is on its way
items.summary_auto: Approved claims started getting paid out during the week of May 11, according to the webpage for the class-action settlement. Depending on whether they received a Loblaw gift card in 2018, Canadians who applied can expect to see either $49.11 or $24.11.

items.title: UN countries agree on legal obligation to address climate change
items.summary_auto: The United Nations General Assembly on Wednesday voted 141–8 to adopt a resolution backing a world court opinion that countries have a legal obligation to address ‌climate change, with the world's biggest historical emitter the United States among those opposing it.

items.title: UN top court finds key labour treaty protects workers' right to strike
items.summary_auto: The International Court of Justice issued on Thursday a landmark advisory opinion that potentially enshrines the right to strike in labour standards and international trade agreements.

items.title: What to expect with this year's Atlantic hurricane season
items.summary_auto: Thanks to the workings of an as-yet-to-form El Niño, the Atlantic basin is expected to see a quieter-than-normal hurricane season.

items.title: This year’s Atlantic hurricane season expected to be below average, thanks to El Niño
items.summary_auto: Thanks to the workings of an as-yet-to-form El Niño, the Atlantic basin is expected to see a quieter-than-normal hurricane season.

items.title: 'What just happened?' Jeff Probst spoils Survivor 50 finale result live on air
items.summary_auto: Oops. Jeff Probst definitely didn’t mean to say that. The longtime Survivor host has just become the latest personality to land in the live-TV blunder hall of fame, making a colossal mistake during Wednesday night’s Survivor 50 finale.

items.title: Stellantis refreshing North American products — and building 11 new models by 2030
items.summary_auto: For North America, the plan is designed to expand the automaker’s hybrid offerings, and offer new pickup trucks, a small van and seven “affordable” vehicles.

items.title: Lebanese bury victims of deadliest Israeli strike since ceasefire was announced
items.summary_auto: Mourners gathered in a southern Lebanese town on Thursday to bury victims from an Israeli airstrike earlier this week that killed 14 people, the deadliest single bombing raid on Lebanon since the announcement of a tenuous ceasefire last month.

items.title: Family of Brampton teen found dead in Saskatchewan devastated, searching for answers: advocate
items.summary_auto: The family of a Brampton, Ont., teen is devastated and looking for answers after he was found dead near a remote Saskatchewan community, a Toronto advocate says.

items.title: Supreme Court of Canada to hear B.C. challenge over mineral rights and DRIPA
items.summary_auto: The Supreme Court of Canada will hear British Columbia's appeal of a ruling that found the United Nations Declaration on the Rights of Indigenous Peoples and the provincial mineral claims regime are "inconsistent."

items.title: RCMP watchdog says Mounties mishandled sex assault case of murdered N.S. woman
items.summary_auto: Nearly 10 years after a Nova Scotia woman was murdered by her neighbour after she reported that he sexually assaulted and harassed her, the RCMP’s national watchdog says the Mounties failed both in the original case, and making improvements to sexual assault investigations across Canada.

items.title: Nixon goes to finance, LaGrange swaps health roles in Alberta cabinet shuffle
items.summary_auto: Alberta Premier Danielle Smith reassigned about one-quarter of her front bench in Calgary on Thursday, including several key ministers.

items.title: IRS settlement preventing Trump-related audits, controversial fund tough to challenge in court, experts say
items.summary_auto: Opponents of President Donald Trump's sweeping legal settlement with the U.S. Internal Revenue Service will face high hurdles in challenging its $1.776-billion US fund for victims of alleged political "weaponization" and its provision barring audits of his taxes, according to legal experts.

items.title: Alberta to hold consultation sessions this year on future plans for dealing with oilsands tailings ponds
items.summary_auto: The Alberta government has announced it will be launching engagement sessions this year as it develops a long-term plan to manage tailings ponds and mine wastewater from the oilsands.

items.title: Queen Elizabeth pressed for Andrew to be U.K. trade envoy, documents reveal
items.summary_auto: Documents show Queen Elizabeth was "very keen" that Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, the former prince, be given the job of U.K. trade envoy, as the U.K. government on Thursday released the confidential papers related to his appointment in 2001, which later proved controversial due to his links to American sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

items.title: Israel deports all Gaza flotilla activists after outcry over their detention
items.summary_auto: Gaza activists who were detained by Israel, and later pinned to the ground to the taunts of the country's far-right police minister, have been released from prison and sent home.

items.title: New Brunswick teen becomes first patient of improved imaging technology in Canada
items.summary_auto: Molly Johnston, 14, is now recovering from spinal surgery that will help treat her scoliosis. She received care at the IWK Health Centre in Halifax, and in the process, became the first patient in Canada to undergo surgery that used an improved 3D imaging machine, according to a news release from the children's hospital.

items.title: My phone was one of thousands snatched in London this year. Like many others, it was trafficked to China
items.summary_auto: London's Metropolitan Police are trying to crack down on sophisticated criminal networks that are snatching phones off the streets of London and exporting them in bulk to China.

items.title: Police killed or injured their loved ones. Grief turned these 3 strangers into allies
items.summary_auto: No one tells you what to do after your loved one is killed or injured in a police intervention. It’s part of what united a handful of families from across Quebec. Together, they’re pushing a shared goal: more transparency and integrity on the part of Quebec’s police watchdog.

items.title: What does Colbert's exit mean for the future of late night?
items.summary_auto: After 11 seasons, the curtains are closing on The Late Show with Stephen Colbert. Industry watchers say while Colbert's voice will be missed, the late-night format needs to change to survive in the streaming era — a transformation that may already be underway.

items.title: Tribunal into missing Indigenous children and unmarked graves to hold sessions in Montreal
items.summary_auto: The Permanent Peoples' Tribunal will be in Montreal May 25-29 to investigate human rights violations related to missing Indigenous children and unmarked graves associated with residential schools.

items.title: Trump purged his Republican critics. Here's why he could soon regret it
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump is celebrating back-to-back victories in his push to oust his critics from the Republican Party. But there are clear signs that his revenge campaign could backfire.

items.title: Cuba braces for possible U.S. military action as uncertainty follows Castro indictment
items.summary_auto: Former Cuban president Raúl Castro will be "keeping his head down," experts say, as questions swirl over whether the U.S. plans to take him by force and bring him to American soil to face charges over an incident from three decades ago. Federal prosecutors announced criminal charges against Castro Wednesday in the 1996 downing of two small planes flown by Miami-based exile group Brothers to the Rescue.

items.title: Dr. Strangelove diplomacy: How the Pentagon’s symbolic defence board freeze with Canada could backfire
items.summary_auto: Washington’s decision to suspend a symbolic Cold War-era defence body may have been meant to pressure Canada on military policy. Instead, experts say it risks reinforcing Canadians' distrust of the United States, complicates the politics of NORAD and missile defence, and makes future purchases of American military equipment even harder for Ottawa to sell domestically.

items.title: What tech CEOs want from the new federal AI strategy
items.summary_auto: As the federal government finishes its delayed national AI strategy, some technology leaders in Canada are hopeful the new policy will resemble an industrial blueprint that illustrates Ottawa is serious about rolling up its sleeves to help the sector build in expedient fashion.

items.title: Northern Ontario health authority's ex-CEO, former chief of staff raise concerns after baby's death
items.summary_auto: A former CEO for the Weeneebayko Area Health Authority says the organization had achieved accreditation before he left in 2010 and he's disappointed it's no longer in place, especially after hearing of the death of an infant at the Fort Albany Hospital. "It really hits home when it’s a young boy that’s not even a year old that passes,” Pat Chilton told CBC News.

items.title: U.S.-bound flight from Paris diverts to Montreal over Ebola restrictions
items.summary_auto: An Air France flight from Paris to Detroit was diverted to Montreal Wednesday night after United States authorities barred it from entering the U.S., citing new restrictions for people who who have recently travelled to three East Africa countries, where a deadly Ebola outbreak has taken hold.

items.title: Montreal Victoire win Walter Cup, beating Ottawa 4-0 to become 1st Canadian PWHL champion
items.summary_auto: Abby Roque scored twice to lead the Montreal Victoire to the team's first Walter Cup trophy Wednesday night with a 4-0 victory over the Ottawa Charge.

items.title: Canada’s questions for the Artemis II crew
items.summary_auto: The Artemis II crew (Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, Jeremy Hansen) meets up with CBC News chief correspondent Adrienne Arsenault in Montreal to answer questions from Canadians about their historic million-kilometre mission around the moon.

items.title: Grizzly feeding videos and black bear attack prompt bear safety reminders in B.C.
items.summary_auto: B.C. 's Conservation Officer Service is reminding people to avoid attracting bears into human habitat or luring them with food, after a pair of incidents in the province this week.

items.title: Bear killed in Mission following attack; Grizzlies allegedly being fed near Smithers
items.summary_auto: B.C. 's Conservation Officer Service is reminding people to avoid attracting bears into human habitat or luring them with food, after a pair of incidents in the province this week.
